name: Arbitrary File Write via Path Traversal in scenario_id Parameter of /api/scenario-improvements/accept
description:
The POST /api/scenario-improvements/accept endpoint accepts a `scenario_id` field in the JSON request body that is used directly in filesystem path construction without any sanitization or validation. The server constructs a path by joining the application directory, "scenarios", and the user-supplied `scenario_id`, then creates directories and writes attacker-controlled content (from the `script_fix` parameter) to a file at that location.

By including path traversal sequences (e.g., `../static/`), an unauthenticated attacker can escape the intended `scenarios/` directory and write arbitrary content to any writable location on the server. When targeting the Flask static directory, the written files become immediately web-accessible, enabling creation of web shells or serving of malicious content.

The endpoint requires no authentication, making this exploitable by any remote attacker. The `script_fix` content is written as-is with no restrictions on content type or size. Combined with the application running as root (uid=0), this vulnerability enables full server compromise through arbitrary file write capabilities.
expected_root_cause: PATH_TRAVERSAL 취약점 — Arbitrary File Write via Path Traversal in scenario_id Parameter of /api/scenario-improvements/accept
